title: Rootless podman unable to use existing rootless network namespace
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Running a rootless podman container with "--net=ns:/proc/$PID/net/ns" fails with permission denied.  The specified network namespace is a rootless network namespace.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create rootless network namespace
```
[test_user@arch-host ~]$ unshare -uUnr /bin/bash
[root@arch-host ~]# hostname namespace
[root@arch-host ~]# echo $$
59142
```
2. Run podman specifying "--net=ns:/proc/$PID/net/ns" using pid from above
```
[test_user@arch-host ~]$ podman run -it --rm --net=ns:/proc/59142/ns/net --name bash docker.io/bash
Error: cannot find specified network namespace path: stat /proc/59142/ns/net: permission denied
```

3. Verify the namespace can be joined with nsenter
```
[test_user@arch-host ~]$ nsenter -U -u -n --preserve-credentials --target 59142 /bin/bash
[root@namespace ~]# hostname
namespace
[root@namespace ~]# ip a
1: lo: <LOOPBACK> mtu 65536 qdisc noop state DOWN group default qlen 1000
    link/loopback 00:00:00:00:00:00 brd 00:00:00:00:00:00
```

4. Verify slirp4netns can join the namespace
```
[test_user@arch-host ~]$ slirp4netns --configure --mtu=65520 --disable-host-loopback 59142 tap0
sent tapfd=5 for tap0
received tapfd=5
Starting slirp
* MTU:             65520
* Network:         10.0.2.0
* Netmask:         255.255.255.0
* Gateway:         10.0.2.2
* DNS:             10.0.2.3
* Recommended IP:  10.0.2.100
```
Now from existing nsenter session in step 3:
```
[root@namespace ~]# ip a
1: lo: <LOOPBACK,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 65536 qdisc noqueue state UNKNOWN group default qlen 1000
    link/loopback 00:00:00:00:00:00 brd 00:00:00:00:00:00
    inet 127.0.0.1/8 scope host lo
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 ::1/128 scope host
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
2: tap0: <BROADCAST,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 65520 qdisc fq_codel state UNKNOWN group default qlen 1000
    link/ether ea:7c:4b:8e:91:cb brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
    inet 10.0.2.100/24 brd 10.0.2.255 scope global tap0
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 fe80::e87c:4bff:fe8e:91cb/64 scope link
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
```

**Describe the results you received:**

podman fails to run the container

**Describe the results you expected:**

podman runs the container in the context of the existing rootless network namespace

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
[test_user@arch-host ~]$ podman version
Version:      3.1.2
API Version:  3.1.2
Go Version:   go1.16.3
Git Commit:   51b8ddbc22cf5b10dd76dd9243924aa66ad7db39
Built:        Wed Apr 21 17:34:03 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
[test_user@arch-host ~]$ pacman -Qi podman
Name            : podman
Version         : 3.1.2-1

Architecture    : x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

Past issues #7816 and #4473 are related but this is not a network namespace created by root.  This network namespace is created by the user running the podman container.  Other tools (nsenter, slirp4netns) seem capable of functioning as expected.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Physical host

comments.body: Can you access `/proc/59142/ns/net`? It looks like a simple `stat` check is failing with permission denied.
The network namespace must be a child of the podman user namespace. Use `podman unshare unshare -n` to create the network namespace.

> The network namespace must be a child of the podman user namespace. Use `podman unshare unshare -n` to create the network namespace.

when use `podman unshare unshare -n` , I can not change the hostname in rootless mode.   is there a way to start with a fixed hostname ?
Hostname requires a UTS namespace as well. `unshare -n -u` should work

Thanks for explain, with `unshare -n -u` allow me set hostname.

I guess this is ns the child of "podman unshare --rootless-cni ip a".

 how can i get the pid of "podman unshare --rootless-cni ip a", so I can modify it with root user like this:

```sh
ip -n ${rootless-cni-pid} route add default dev  wg1 
```
This is not related to this issue. Please open a new discussion instead of commenting on old issues: https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions
